identity.canonical_title: Compact Worked Example (Explicit Composite Streamfunction): ω_3,2≈ -2Ω· 2 /(3·4)

theory.canonical_statement: # 5) Compact worked example (explicit composite streamfunction) Take \[ \Psi(\theta,\lambda,t)=A_d\cos\theta \;+\; \frac{\Gamma_N}{4\pi}\ln(1-\cos\theta) \;+\; B\;Y_{3}^{2}(\theta,\lambda)\,e^{i\omega_{3,2} t}, \] with Earth-like parameters \(R=6.371\times10^6\ \mathrm{m}\), \(\Omega=7.2921\times10^{-5}\ \mathrm{rad/s}\). - The dipole component sets large-scale opposite hemisphere pull. - The \(\Gamma_N\) term concentrates an additional north-hemisphere pull (north-arm stronger). - The \(Y_3^2\) term creates a 3–lobed pattern modulated in time (wave undulation). Use \(\omega_{3,2}\approx -2\Omega\cdot 2 /(3\cdot4) = -\tfrac{4\Omega}{12} = -\tfrac{\Omega}{3}\) (approx) to get sense of rotation.
theory.technical_interpretation: Compact Worked Example (Explicit Composite Streamfunction): ω_3,2≈ -2Ω· 2 /(3·4) is a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation, written ω_3,2≈ -2Ω· 2 /(3·4) = -tfrac4Ω12 = -tfracΩ3. It expresses a scaling or proportionality, capturing how the quantity grows with its parameters. It is built from Ω, defining ω_3,2≈ -2Ω· 2 /(3·4). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where the term creates a 3–lobed pattern modulated in time (wave undulation).
